title: A.1 enforcement-status flip + end-to-end smoke test
goal: Flip HANDSHAKE_BUILD_RULES.json.enforcement.implementation_status from DESIGN_ONLY_WIRING_PENDING to ACTIVE; write the end-to-end smoke test that exercises MT-001..MT-008 in a single live run against the KERNEL-004 packet itself; confirm every cluster A.1 acceptance criterion is met by code, not by prose.
expected_diff_shape: One-line registry status flip + version bump in HANDSHAKE_BUILD_RULES.json (version_log entry added); new end-to-end test src/backend/handshake_core/tests/hbr_e2e_smoke_test.rs that orchestrates loader -> hydrator -> matrix-check -> handoff-gate -> violation-emit in one run.
unblock_conditions: MT-002 through MT-008 merged; PROVED evidence_pointers exist for AC-HBR-AUTHORITY / AC-HBR-HANDOFF-GATE / AC-HBR-TYPED-RECEIPT / AC-PROCESS-LEDGER-* via their respective MT tests.
implementation_notes: Two activities. (1) Registry update: change `enforcement.implementation_status` from `"DESIGN_ONLY_WIRING_PENDING"` to `"ACTIVE"`; bump `version` from `1.1.0` to `1.2.0`; add a new `version_log` entry `{ version: "1.2.0", date: "<today>", summary: "Enforcement wiring active per MT-001..MT-008. PACKET_ACCEPTANCE_MATRIX auto-emission, gov-check sub-check, validator-scan integration, handoff-gate evaluator, ProcessOwnershipLedger reclaim, and HBR_VIOLATION typed receipts all online.", rgf: null, changeset: "WP-KERNEL-004-MT-009" }`. The `enforcement.implementation_owner` text becomes a past-tense reference to MT-001..MT-008 and removes the 'Until that wiring lands' phrase. (2) End-to-end test: in a single cargo test, instantiate an in-memory Postgres test fixture (use sqlx-test or testcontainers per repo convention; confirm via Grep before authoring), run hbr-matrix-hydrate against a fixture packet, run hbr-matrix-check (via std::process::Command shelling to node), run a synthetic HandoffGate::evaluate, deliberately fail one rule, assert an HBR_VIOLATION is emitted in canonical-JSON to a captured sink, assert ProcessOwnershipLedger records a START + STOP pair for the synthetic test process, assert that the gov-check umbrella exit code reflects the failure. This smoke test is the wire test that proves the entire A.1 chain is live, satisfying the HBR-INT-001 evidence_kind=test_run_with_ledger_replay obligation for the KERNEL-004 packet itself. NOTE: Because changing HANDSHAKE_BUILD_RULES.json from DESIGN_ONLY_WIRING_PENDING to ACTIVE flips enforcement globally, this MT must land AFTER all of MT-001..MT-008 are merged and green; otherwise repository-wide gov-check fails everywhere.
risk_if_missed: Without the flip and smoke test, A.1 ships as nine wired-but-not-active MTs; the registry remains advisory.
validator_focus: End-to-end test exercises the WHOLE chain in one run (not eight isolated unit tests); registry flip happens in the same commit as the smoke test pass so the repo cannot be in an inconsistent state.

minimum_controls: Registry flip and smoke test land in the SAME commit so the repo is never in a partially-active state.
Smoke test exercises the failure path (not just the happy path) and asserts a typed HBR_VIOLATION row is produced.
version_log entry is appended (never replaces an existing entry).
